findings.suggestion: Narrow the handler to `except json.JSONDecodeError` (and/or `ValueError`) so control-flow exceptions propagate. Either re-raise/wrap parse failures (e.g. raise a ConfigError) or make the failure explicit to callers instead of returning a silent `{}`; if a default is truly desired, log the error and document that `{}` means 'parse failed'.
findings.summary: source.py:8 uses a bare `except:` which catches BaseException — including KeyboardInterrupt and SystemExit — so Ctrl-C and interpreter shutdown are suppressed inside parse_config. It also returns `{}` (line 11) on *any* failure, and load_settings (line 16) passes that straight back, so a malformed or unreadable config silently degrades to empty settings with no signal to callers. The `-> dict` return type is structurally honored but its semantic contract (a valid parsed config) is broken, and the inline comment (lines 8–10) confirms this is a known BUG that was committed rather than fixed.
findings.title: Bare except in parse_config swallows BaseException and silently returns {}

findings.suggestion: Catch only json.JSONDecodeError (or ValueError) and re-raise / propagate rather than swallowing. Either let load_settings raise on unparseable config so the caller can show an explicit error state, or return a sentinel (e.g. None) plus the raw error so callers can distinguish parse failure from an intentionally empty config. Never map 'parse failed' to the same value as 'valid empty object'.
findings.summary: source.py:6-11: the bare `except:` in parse_config catches everything (including BaseException) and returns `{}`. Via load_settings (source.py:14-16), a malformed/truncated config file therefore resolves to an empty settings dict with no error, log, or empty-state signal — the data-loss/confusing-state pattern where a user's persisted settings silently appear reset and callers cannot distinguish 'missing' from 'corrupt' from 'legitimately empty'. Blast radius is 0 callers today (both symbols unused), so this is latent, not a live regression — hence medium, not high.
findings.title: parse_config silently returns {} on any parse failure → load_settings yields empty settings with no error

findings.suggestion: Add test_source.py asserting parse_config on valid JSON round-trips and on invalid JSON raises (not returns {}), then run `python -m pytest -q` and attach the output. This is optional cleanup relative to the bare-except fix but resolves the blocked rerun_relevant_verification gate.
findings.summary: source.py adds parse_config and load_settings (0 callers, newly added) with no accompanying tests and no fresh command/test output in history — matching the pre-review `no-fresh-verification-evidence` candidate. Nothing proves the intended behavior (surface parse errors vs. return {}), so the change is only statically plausible.
findings.title: New parse/load functions ship with no tests or verification evidence

summary:
Risk-tiered review (lite) completed.

Single new 16-line file demonstrating a bare-except antipattern. The code contains a genuine, self-documented bug: `except:` at line 8 catches BaseException (KeyboardInterrupt/SystemExit) and returns `{}` on any failure, which load_settings propagates — masking parse failures from callers. The author's own comment agrees it's a BUG, and the change shipped with it unfixed. No live regression yet (0 callers, newly added), but the defect is real and would mislead future callers.

Single 16-line new file with one genuine defect in product-safety scope: parse_config swallows all exceptions and returns {}, so load_settings turns a corrupt/truncated config into a silent empty-settings state (potential user-visible data loss, no error surfaced). Capped at medium because both functions currently have 0 callers (confirmed via grep, matches blast-radius data) — the defect is real and certain but latent until wired into a flow. The bare `except:` also catches KeyboardInterrupt/SystemExit, but json.loads is fast so that sub-issue is minor. load_settings does not guard open() errors, so OSError propagates raw — an inconsistent contract worth noting, not a data-loss bug.

Small 16-line new file with one certain defect: the bare `except:` at line 8 catches BaseException (swallowing KeyboardInterrupt/SystemExit) and returns `{}` on any failure, so parse errors are invisible to callers including load_settings (line 16). Both functions are currently uncalled (0 callers), which caps real-world impact and keeps this at medium rather than high. No tests or fresh verification exist, so the change is unproven.
